title: Suregada procera
intro: Suregada procera, also known as the "Tall Ironwood", is a medium-sized tree native to tropical Africa and the Indian Ocean. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Suregada procera include flavonoids, terpenoids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include decoctions, infusions, and ointments, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including skin disorders and fever.
taxonomy: Suregada procera is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Euphorbiaceae. It is a tropical tree native to the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia. The Euphorbiaceae family is a large and diverse group of plants that includes many species of trees and shrubs. The classification of Suregada procera is based on its botanical characteristics, including its leaf morphology and fruit structure. This classification system is widely accepted by botanists and is used to identify and categorize plant species.
